Plaintiffs Watchanaporn Changto And Saksee Isanpayu'S Omnibus Motion For Discovery Relief And Request For Monetary Sanctions (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFFS WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO and SAKSEE ISANPAYU'S Omnibus Motion For Discovery Relief And Request For Monetary Sanctions (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only) is a duplicate entry. (302)

Ntc Of Mtn And Omnibus Mtn For Discovery Relief And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Sean Tamura-Sato, Esq. of Minami Tamaki LLP, Tel. 415.788.9000, for plaintiff and moving party;
Susan E. Bishop, Esq. of Berliner Cohen, Tel. 408.286.5800, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification. PLAINTIFFS WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO and SAKSEE ISANPAYU'S Omnibus Motion For Discovery Relief And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows: 1. Defendants 2001 Thai Stick, Inc ("THAI STICK") and Polemahasuppapole ( "PAUL POLE") (collectively "defendants") objections to form interrogatories nos. 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.7 & 2.8 are overruled and they will serve full and complete answers, without objection to said inteerogatories. 2. As to form interrogatories nos. 2.5, 2.11, 2.12, 2.13, 3.7, 4.1, 4.2, 7.5, 9.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1 & 15.1, defendants did respond. Plaintiffs have made no showing that the responses are incomplete other than to argue that the objections were untimely and without merit. The court will make no further order requiring defendants to further respond. 3. Thai Stick is ORDERED to serve verified responses without objection to all of plaintiffs' requests for admission which responses will be in the form specified in CCP - admit, deny or express an inability to either admit or deny after reasonable inquiry. 4. To the extent that any of Thai Stick's responses to the requests for admission are other than an outright admission, Thai Stick will serve without objections full and complete responses to form interrogatory 17.1 and in so doing shall correctly identify the request for admission to which its resopnses apply. 5. Thai Stick is ORDERED to serve full and complete responses without objection responses to special interrogatories nos. 1, 2 & 20 as to any employee employed by any restaurant owned by defendant Thai Stick during the period of plaintiffs' employment. 6. To the extent it has not already done so, Thai Stick will produce all of the documents requested in Plaintiffs' Request for Production of Documents with the exception of Request for Production Nos. 5, 6, 25, 35, 41, 42, 47, 48, 49 & 52 which can await a determination as to the appropriate class, if any, in this litigation. 7. Monetary sanctions will be imposed as against defendants and their counsel in the amount of $2,500 to be paid to plaintiffs. 8. Defendants shall serve further responses, produce documents and pay sanctions within 10 days from the date of this ORDER. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
